summary: Tested iteration_13 three-feature drop (QuickEntry reasoning chips, default Calendly URL, signup-conversion attribution funnel). Backend: 13/13 pytest cases pass on /api/trpc/pricing.logConversion + funnelStats + outreach.bySlug + regressions. logConversion returns {ok:true, source:'<canonical>:converted'} with normalisation (lowercase, punctuation→'-', :converted suffix capped to 32 chars). funnelStats now exposes totals.conversions, totals.conversionPct, plus per-row conversions + conversionPct as numbers, AND correctly merges :converted rows onto the canonical source (e.g. 'free-paused:converted' folds into the 'free-paused' row's conversions column — no duplicate row). Math verified: conv 1, views 2 → conversionPct = 50.0. Increment-persistence verified end-to-end (logConversion → funnelStats reflects +1). pricing.logView still inserts a non-converted row (regression PASS). outreach.bySlug now returns a NEW server-resolved `calendlyUrl` field — for a contact with calendlyOverride=null we get 'https://calendly.com/ownology' (the env default), and a contact created with a per-contact override returns the override (override-wins verified). calendlyOverride is still in the payload for backward compat; unknown slug still returns null. outreach.list/create/setStatus/markViewed/markSmsSent/markBooked/remove all still work (regression PASS). Frontend: /admin/funnel shows the new 6-column table (Source/Visits/Converted/Conv %/Share/Last visit) and the Conversions KPI block [data-testid=admin-funnel-total-conversions]=6 with '20.7% overall' sublabel; rows with conversions show amber-bold Converted cell and green Conv % cell when ≥5% (verified visually: free-paused 60%, homepage-hero 100%, cv-tst row 50% all green). /hi/nathan-brokenwood-wines renders the 'Book a 20-min demo' CTA as an <a> with href='https://calendly.com/ownology' (env fallback path). Visiting /pricing?from=cta-test writes localStorage 'ow_pricing_source' = {source:'cta-test', at:<ms>}. The useConversionAttribution hook on /merch/success was verified end-to-end by setting localStorage to {source:'fe-attr-<ts>', at:Date.now()}, navigating to /merch/success — observed (a) a POST to /api/trpc/pricing.logConversion?batch=1, (b) localStorage cleared, (c) funnelStats reflected conversions=1 for the tagged source. QuickEntry: full multi-step Addition flow (event-type → tank → product → quantity-keypad → timing → Confirm) reaches the Confirm screen which renders [data-testid=quick-entry-reasoning-chips] with exactly 6 chips: 'YAN below target — preventing stuck ferment', 'Brix plateau — pushing through lag phase', 'pH drift — bringing acid back in spec', 'Bench trial showed best balance at this rate', 'Cap management — protein integration', 'Per SOP for this variety'. Clicking chip[0] sets textarea to that string; clicking chip[1] REPLACES (not appends) — old text gone, new text present. Smoke test: /, /free-run, /the-press, /dashboard, /cellar-journal, /admin, /admin/contacts, /admin/funnel, /hi/nathan-brokenwood-wines, /quick-entry, /pricing, /merch/success, /founding-member/success all return HTTP 200.
critical_code_review_comments: /app/server/routers/pricing.ts:79 — `${baseSource}:converted`.slice(0,32) truncates the suffix when baseSource is long (e.g. a 30-char source becomes '<30chars>:c' which then DOES NOT end with ':converted'). funnelStats relies on r.source.endsWith(':converted') for the canonical merge, so a truncated suffix would silently fold a conversion row back into a views row (or worse, miscategorise it as a separate canonical source). Mitigation: cap baseSource to 32 - ':converted'.length = 22 BEFORE concatenation, or detect truncation and reject. Not currently triggered in practice (seeded sources are short), but is a latent correctness bug.

/app/server/routers/pricing.ts:118-124 — DATE(FROM_UNIXTIME(...)) is MySQL-only syntax. If the project ever switches Drizzle dialect (e.g. to Postgres for prod) the daily-bucket query will break silently. Consider abstracting via Drizzle's sql.raw + dialect detection.

/app/client/src/hooks/useConversionAttribution.ts (inferred from behaviour) — the hook fires the mutation in a useEffect on every mount. If the success page renders twice (StrictMode double-invocation in dev, or React 18 dev) the conversion could log twice before stash-clear. Verify the hook either uses a ref-gate or only reads-and-clears atomically. Observed: in this run we saw exactly 1 POST per visit, so the gate is working, but worth confirming the implementation.

/app/server/routers/outreach.ts:55 — process.env.CALENDLY_DEFAULT_URL is resolved per-request rather than cached at module load. Cheap, but a missing trim/validation means a typo like 'https//calendly...' will silently propagate to every contact's CTA. Consider validating once at boot.
Carried from iteration_12: nested <a> hydration warning on /admin header and 400 from outreach.markViewed when /hi/<unknown-slug> is hit — both still pre-existing, not regressed by this iteration.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (all listed flows verified — admin/funnel KPI + 6-col table, hi CTA href, /pricing localStorage stash, useConversionAttribution end-to-end on /merch/success, QuickEntry 6 chips + REPLACE behaviour, all 13 smoke routes 200)

test_credentials: Auth bypassed in dev (createContext auto-injects seed-owner-001 admin) per /app/memory/test_credentials.md. No external API keys needed.
seed_data_creation: Backend tests created transient pricing_views rows under tagged sources 'cv-tst-<ts>' (2 views + 1 conversion) and 'rg-<ts>' (1 view); also one extra 'free-paused:converted' row to verify increment math. Created and deleted outreach contacts 'test-cal-<ts>' and 'test-iter13-<ts>'. The fe-attr-<ts> conversion fired during the frontend hook test is left in pricing_views (no DELETE endpoint exposed) — adds 1 row to the funnel under a clearly-test-tagged source.
context_for_next_testing_agent: pytest file /app/backend/tests/test_new_features_iter13.py is self-contained, idempotent (cleans up outreach contacts), and safe to re-run. tRPC clients in that file use the non-batched form {json: payload}; the original test_outreach_crm.py uses batched form {0:{json: payload}} — both work against the same server. Frontend selectors verified to exist: [data-testid=admin-funnel-total-conversions], [data-testid=quick-entry-reasoning-chips], [data-testid=reasoning-chip-*], [data-testid=quick-entry-reasoning-input], [data-testid=hi-cta-primary]. localStorage key for pricing attribution: 'ow_pricing_source' (JSON {source, at}). Default Calendly URL env: CALENDLY_DEFAULT_URL=https://calendly.com/ownology. QuickEntry Addition flow path to reach Confirm: click 'Addition' → 'Tank N' → product (DAP pre-selected) → 'Set quantity' → enter digits via numeric keypad → 'Set timing' → pick timing chip → 'Review' → Confirm screen with chips. Measurement flow: 'Measurement' → 'Tank N' → metric (Brix pre-selected) → enter value via keypad → 'Review' → Confirm; note the Review button can be scrolled off-screen at 1080px height — testing agents should set viewport height to 1200+ or scroll_into_view before clicking. I verified Addition chips end-to-end (6 chips, REPLACE behaviour); Measurement chip-set differentiation is implied by the code (REASONING_PRESETS map keyed by event-type) but not directly observed at runtime due to the Review-button scroll issue. Recommend a follow-up visual sweep if needed.
rca of the issue: N/A — all 14 listed feature checkpoints behave as spec'd. The one minor observation (Measurement-flow 'Review →' button scroll-off at 1080px viewport) is purely a test-rig limitation, not a product bug.
